title: lathyrus nervosus
intro: Lathyrus nervosus, also known as wild pea or annual pea, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the legume family, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and small, edible seeds. Traditionally, Lathyrus nervosus has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to the presence of compounds like saponins and alkaloids. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable ingredient in traditional medicine. Lathyrus nervosus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin conditions.
